expectations.evidence: File uses structured blocks: module, entity, value, command, event, aggregate, enum, reaction, temporal-event keywords throughout
11 entities: Clinic, Veterinarian, AvailabilitySlot, Appointment, PetOwner, Pet, Consultation, MedicalRecordShare, ClinicSearchResult, Review, NotificationRecord
Multiple value types: Address, GeoCoordinate, TimeSlot, ContactInfo, Rating, OpeningHours, Specialty, TeamMember, RecurrencePattern, CancellationDetail, VaccinationRecord, ConsultationSummary
RegisterClinic, PublishClinicProfile, BookAppointment, CancelAppointment, RegisterPetOwner, CompleteAppointment, MarkAppointmentNoShow, etc.
ClinicRegistered, ClinicProfilePublished, AppointmentBooked, AppointmentCancelled, AppointmentCompleted, PetOwnerRegistered, VeterinarianRegistered, etc.

No 'statemachine' blocks found. Transitions are defined inline within entity blocks using 'transitions {}' syntax (lines 267, 421, 860), which is a different pattern — not a standalone statemachine block

Multiple invariants: publishedClinicHasRequiredFields, slotDateInFuture, bookedSlotCannotBeModified, cancelledAppointmentHasDetail, completedAppointmentHasTimestamp, cancellationWindowRespected, ownerMustConsentToGdpr
Error events: BookingFailed (line 1078), CancellationRejected (line 1086), PetOwnerRegistrationFailed; preconditions on cancellation (24h window)
No 'database', 'API', or 'DTO' found in entity or value type names
All satisfies clauses are empty arrays: 'satisfies []' throughout the file, consistent with source being a PRD with no formal requirement IDs
5 bounded contexts as modules: ClinicManagement, AppointmentBooking, PetHealthRecord, ClinicDiscovery, Notification
